description: Horizontal stacked-bar chart comparing keypress latency budgets for contact switches and optical switches. Contact switch bar (top) shows three proportional segments: bounce (1–5ms, terracotta fill), debounce window (5ms, warm grey fill), and polling (1ms, dark fill) — total ~6–11ms. Optical switch bar (bottom) shows polling only (1ms, dark fill) — total ~1ms. A ghost outline of the contact-switch bar width sits behind the optical bar so the eye can compare the scale directly. Bars are visually proportional: scale of 58px per ms so the full contact bar spans 640px. Legend strip below the chart identifies the three fill types.

data_sources.content: article brief specifying: contact switch bounce 1–5ms, debounce window 5ms, polling 1ms, total ~6–11ms; optical switch polling 1ms only, total ~1ms
